title: on chan(payload) heads
manager: Transition heads that bind a typed payload — `on c(payload) ->` — so a transition can name and use the data an event carries, not just react to its name. This is what makes typed channels actually useful: the payload flows into the transition's guards and assignments as a bound, typed value, so "on deposit(amount)" can reference the amount instead of the event having to stash its data somewhere on the side.
cs: `on c(payload) ->` heads bind typed channel payloads (fsl#1348); the payload is available to guards/assigns; per-channel type-checked.
